official.name: Brian P. Kemp
official.office_address: Office of the Governor, Atlanta, Georgia

summary.narrative: Across this term, Kemp delivered on his major fiscal and education-funding commitments: he completed and exceeded the $5,000 teacher pay raise, fully funded the school formula, cut the state income tax below five percent, returned billions in surplus rebates, and maintained Georgia's AAA bond rating. He also signed anti-gang and human-trafficking legislation and advanced literacy initiatives. Most tracked promises show completed legislative or budgetary action; the literacy promise is advanced but lacks documented outcome metrics, and the CRT-related promise has limited and contested evidence.
summary.grade_rationale: The record shows a high proportion of fully delivered, clearly specified, statewide promises with documented legislative and budgetary action driven by the official, supporting strong delivery scores. A small number of promises rest on thinner or contested evidence or lack confirmed outcome measurement, which moderates confidence on those items but does not change the predominant pattern of completed delivery.

data_gaps: Many promise candidates were biographical pages, Senate-race speculation, or debate logistics and were excluded. The CRT/divisive-ideologies promise (c5) lacks a specifically cited signed bill in the delivery material and is contested by third parties; literacy delivery lacks measurable outcome data. Negative promise (bond rating) assigned binary D4 per rubric. Some delivery evidence dated 2026 falls within term (ends 2027-01-09). Healthcare/Medicaid-related material exists (Pathways) but no clear campaign-era promise was isolated from it.

provisional_status.display: Grade is record-to-date — term in progress, 86.2% elapsed (as of 2026-06-22). It will change as the term continues.
provisional_status.note: Distinct from any per-promise 'PROVISIONAL (early-term)' flag in scorecard.card_flags, which concerns a single promise's own deadline, not the term's completeness.
